chosen.headline: Sensex, Nifty close ~0.3% lower as IT selloff overpowers broad-market gains

story.headline: Sensex, Nifty close ~0.3% lower as IT selloff overpowers broad-market gains
story.what_happened: Indian benchmark indices ended lower in a volatile session on the last trading day of June. The Sensex fell 249.70 points (-0.33%) to 76,478.67 and the Nifty 50 dropped 80.50 points (-0.34%) to 23,865.75, while the Nifty Bank index eased 184.45 points (-0.32%) to 57,542.90. Despite the headline weakness, market breadth was positive with roughly 2,250 advances against 1,805 declines, and the Nifty Midcap 100 (+0.4%) and Smallcap 100 (+1%) outperformed.
story.why_it_matters: The headline drop masked a two-speed market: heavyweight IT exporters dragged the benchmarks while domestic-facing sectors (realty, consumer durables, autos) and broader midcap/smallcap segments held firm, signaling that the weakness was sector-specific rather than a broad risk-off move. The session also marked the close of the June series amid derivatives-expiry volatility.
story.investor_takeaway: For educational context only: a falling headline index alongside positive breadth and midcap/smallcap outperformance reflects concentrated sector pressure (IT) rather than a market-wide decline. Not investment advice.

sources.name: HDFCSky - Stock Market Close Report, June 30, 2026
